item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
nice
as is tradition
i don't wanna mess with it at all
busted
older that that but yeah
it should
those are pretty good
or a 7 year old kid
it me you off them this everything everybody the world
right?
mmm hmm
improvise. adapt. overcome.
who else would it revolve around?
obviously
finally
not sure what vibe this is but i agree
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Screaming into the void for ads on Amazon now
item.description: Tried to post this to the AmazonPrimeVideo reddit day before yesterday but both times I tried the post it got moderated and removed within 60 seconds, even after I re-posted it with the proper spoiler tags. So here it is. (text below) I have a hard time believing that particular reddit is not moderated by Amazon employees now. If you have any streaming service that is injecting ads to the middle of a show, watch out that they're not cutting the show's runtime and replacing it with ads. ______________________ Had a prime subscription for years, enjoyed having the streaming service as part of it, didn't even mind (a little) when a price hike happened a year or so ago. But suddenly getting less service WITH ADS NOW for the same price was a cr@ppy way to go about it. The ads themselves are VERY disruptive to the shows for several reasons. 1. They do not pick a good spot for the show - a venue or clean scene change. They just appear in the middle. 2. They are removing 60-90 seconds of the show each time so the ad can play!! They're not even cleanly breaking the show, the show is continuing to play while the ad plays. I can tell you examples - we are re-watching Rings of Power and only 3 episodes in my wife and I are clearly remembering scenes that happened, or would have been aired during the time the ad was in play. It's breaking up scenes in such a way as to make it very confusing if you haven't seen the show - there's literally dialog missing. S1E2 and E3 in particular - a scene where Elrond and Celebrimbor discussing WHY they were going to the dwarflands was skipped and it just showed them walking up to the door. The scene with Elrond and Durin during the rock-smashing. It cuts away in the middle of a smash (literally) and then the scene that re-appeared when the ad was done was clearly the 60-90 seconds further along, and not a resumption directly of the scene. A scene with Theo (I think that's the kids name) and his friend before they went out to raid for food convincing the friend to go - it was maybe 15-30 second scene but was overplayed by the ad and it skipped to them already out of camp and the friend asking how he was convinced to go. A scene with Galadriel and Elendil in the library before they handed the sigil to the librarian for searching - there was introduction to the scene and relevant conversation. Now, if the ads were all up-front before the show started (they way they do with The Boys) I might not complain, and if ads were intelligently placed at good venue/scene breaks I might live with it - but if they will not even consider that the show needs to pause while the ads play, and then resume they're wrecking their own model. These shows do NOT need to fit into a broadcast slot, a limited time. If the entire run-time is extended by 3-5 minutes by ads, then why is it an issue? Why does Prime have to choose the worst way to play ads?
item.dc:creator: fractalsphere

item.title: Be at peace Little One.
item.description:
She was a great cat. I adopted her as my wife says when I married her 11 years ago as Tiki was her and her kids cat before we met. I'll miss the snuggles she would give at night in bed as I'm sure my daughter does. Tiki seemed to wanted to sleep and cuddle with everyone but couldn't as we don't all sleep in the same bed. For an outdoor cat she lived a long time. Glad the wife could help her and she was able to help her pass in peace.
R.I.P. Tiki. 2006 - 2024
item.dc:creator: StunttheRunt

item.title: It's meme dump day .... Again... Vol 113
item.description:
Hello, it's Wednesday my dudes. Hope it's a good one for you.
I love nandor so much.
.
.
I'm fine with this
Yes.
Oh good.
.
.
.
I feel like some days it's all I think about all day. It's so hard to focus on anything else.
.
.
I'm fine
I had to save this for @vudumommajuju
.
I'll work on myself but I'm not trying to get more sane.
I like being a swamp witch, thank you.
Took you a while
Need
.
Have lived in various cities and there's always at least one
.
.
.
.
Oh noooo, Elmo.
I hate those fucking things.
All hail the raccoon demon God
Hans Christian Anderson books were all sad as fuck.
Always an adventure
Lol
Why is this so adorable
Fuck
Hahaha half my siblings still need to get on antidepressants so definitely not.
Lol
.
The WORST
Foolish mortal
I've never done Ayahuasca but this still made me laugh
.
I need to start doing this to my guy but I don't wanna ruin a good supply lol
I feel like this every day. Fuck chronic fatigue
This election year is an actual nightmare. I know you non Americans are probably sick of hearing about it but holy fuck.
.
.
I mean... Listen to your instincts I guess?
Stand off
He's getting so big and beefy. I posted a meme dump yesterday too for you who like to follow along: https://imgur.com/gallery/3wmOMG0
Happy Wednesday, my dudes. Toad friend says hello

item.title: This has gotta be a record for "World's Fastestest Mental Flip-flop"
item.description:
Context: my local radio station ran a "WHERE'S GREG?!" story this morning (post-Hurricaine Beryl), and MAGA Kim here, RUSHED to Abbott 's defense. See if you can spot the flaw in her logic... it's subtle.
Shut the fuck up, Kim.

Her name is Frankie.

item.dc:creator: nightsider

item.title: Mephistopheles and Margaretta (A two-sided wooden sculpture carved by an unknown French artist, inspired by "Faust")
item.description:
Carved from a block of sycamore wood in the 19th century by an unknown artist, meant to depict the dicthomy of Good & Evil with characters from Johann Wolfgang von Goethe's 1808 play "Faust". The demon Mephistopheles and the innocent girl Gretchen (in other adaptations called Margaretta or Margaret), who was enslaved by the demon after Faust made a pact with him.
The sculpture now resides in the Salar Jung Museum in Hyderabad, India (after it was acquired by the former Prime Minister of Hyderabad Salar Jung I in 1876 during his travels in France.
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel
